title: Add end-to-end tier selection test for MemoryConsolidator
analysis:
The task requires a single integration test that exercises the full path: ReconciliationConfig (sonnet defaults) → _select_tier() → run_full_cycle() → MemoryConsolidator gets episode_limit=125 and memory_limit=250.

Key code paths:
1. ReconciliationConfig (config/schema.py) has sonnet_episode_limit=125, sonnet_memory_limit=250 as defaults.
2. _select_tier() (harness.py:224-239) reads buffer stats; when buffer_count <= buffer_size_threshold * opus_threshold_ratio (10 * 1.5 = 15), it returns a TierConfig with sonnet values.
3. run_full_cycle() (harness.py:352) accepts a tier param (or defaults to TierConfig()).
4. At harness.py:417-421, when iterating stages, if isinstance(stage, MemoryConsolidator), it sets stage.episode_limit = tier.episode_limit and stage.memory_limit = tier.memory_limit.

Existing test_stages.py::TestTierConfig tests are unit-level — they only verify class defaults and mutability but never exercise _select_tier() or run_full_cycle() integration. Existing test_harness.py tests use run_full_cycle but never assert tier limit propagation to MemoryConsolidator.

The new test belongs in test_harness.py because it requires a full ReconciliationHarness with journal/buffer fixtures, matching the existing pattern of harness integration tests. The test will:
1. Mock buffer.get_buffer_stats to return {'size': 0} (well below opus threshold, ensuring sonnet tier).
2. Call harness._select_tier() and assert the returned TierConfig has correct sonnet values.
3. Mock all stage.run() methods with a capture function (pattern from test_full_cycle_extracts_project_root_from_events).
4. Call run_full_cycle() with the returned tier.
5. Assert that the captured MemoryConsolidator state has episode_limit=125 and memory_limit=250.

steps.description: Write failing test test_sonnet_tier_selection_sets_consolidator_limits in test_harness.py. The test: (1) constructs ReconciliationHarness with default ReconciliationConfig, (2) mocks event_buffer.get_buffer_stats to return {'size': 0}, (3) calls _select_tier('test-project') and asserts TierConfig has model='sonnet', episode_limit=125, memory_limit=250, (4) mocks each stage.run with a capture callback (using the existing pattern from test_full_cycle_extracts_project_root_from_events), (5) calls run_full_cycle('test-project', 'buffer_size:2', tier=tier), (6) asserts the captured MemoryConsolidator (stage 0) had episode_limit=125 and memory_limit=250 at call time. Push events to buffer before drain. Import TierConfig from fused_memory.reconciliation.harness.

No implementation code changes needed — this test validates existing behavior. Run pytest on test_harness.py to confirm the test passes. If it fails, debug and fix the test (not the implementation) since sonnet-tier propagation is already implemented in harness.py:417-421.

design_decisions.decision: Place test in test_harness.py rather than test_stages.py
design_decisions.rationale: The test exercises _select_tier() and run_full_cycle() which are harness methods requiring journal/event_buffer fixtures. test_stages.py tests are unit-level stage construction tests without these fixtures. The existing test_harness.py pattern (inline harness construction with mocked stages) is the exact pattern needed.

design_decisions.decision: Mock buffer stats to return {'size': 0} for sonnet tier selection
design_decisions.rationale: Default opus_threshold_ratio=1.5 and buffer_size_threshold=10 means opus triggers at count > 15. A size of 0 guarantees sonnet tier selection deterministically without depending on threshold arithmetic.

This existing test at line 208 demonstrates the exact pattern for capturing stage state at call time within mocked stage.run(). Reusing this pattern maintains test consistency and avoids inventing a new approach.

design_decisions.decision: Assert both _select_tier() return value AND MemoryConsolidator attributes
design_decisions.rationale: The task asks for end-to-end verification. Asserting only the TierConfig wouldn't prove it's applied to MemoryConsolidator. Asserting only stage attributes wouldn't prove _select_tier() produced the right config. Both assertions together prove the full path.

design_decisions.decision: Single test function covers the full e2e path
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly asks for a test that 'exercises the full path from a sonnet-tier ReconciliationConfig through _select_tier()'. Splitting into multiple tests would lose the end-to-end property. One test with clear sections (select tier → run cycle → assert limits) is the right granularity.

reuse.how: Reuse existing fixtures for ReconciliationJournal and EventBuffer setup/teardown
Reuse existing AsyncMock-based memory service with pre-configured method returns
Reuse to create test events to push into the buffer before running the cycle
Adapt the capture callback pattern to also capture episode_limit and memory_limit from the MemoryConsolidator stage
Follow the same pattern: create FusedMemoryConfig with ReconciliationConfig, construct ReconciliationHarness inline
